_comment: Fixture: documented network endpoints exercised by `cargo fetch` / `cargo build`
against an empty cache on Rust 1.70+ (sparse-index default).
Source of truth: cargo 1.70 release notes (sparse protocol default),
This file freezes the set of hosts the `cargo` built-in preset must allow.
`cargo_preset_matches_frozen_trace`) fails if the preset's host list
diverges from this fixture, forcing a conscious update with a
Verification method: spec-documented endpoint enumeration rather than
a live trace — booting a throwaway VM + priming an empty cargo cache
+ running strace on cargo's TLS connections exceeded the M10-S5 time
budget. When a future milestone boots a guest with full outbound
network access, this file should be regenerated from a real pcap /
tcpdump + DNS log pair to catch any undocumented endpoints (e.g. new

hosts.purpose: Sparse-protocol registry index. Default since Rust 1.70.
hosts.notes: Replaces the older git-based registry on crates.io-index.git. Cargo hits this first to resolve versions.

hosts.purpose: Registry web API: search, yank, publish, owner management, and the `/api/v1/crates/<name>/<version>/download` redirector.
hosts.notes: The download endpoint is a 302 that points at `static.crates.io`; cargo follows it to fetch the tarball.

hosts.purpose: CDN host that serves crate tarballs and (historically) RSS feeds.
hosts.notes: Receives the redirected download requests from `crates.io/api/v1/crates/.../download`.
